consistency.class_match: The feature correctly associates with the 'ranger' class.
consistency.level_match: The feature is designated as level 10, which is consistent with standard class progression.
consistency.mechanical_coherence: The mechanics (1 minute casting time, material requirements, +10 Stealth bonus while stationary, limited duration/reapplication) are mechanically sound within the context of D&D 5e rules, emphasizing stationary stealth.

quality.clarity: The description is very clear regarding the preparation time, material components, the specific condition for gaining the bonus (+10 Stealth), and the requirement to reapply the effect.
quality.flavor: The flavor aligns well with the Ranger archetype focusing on wilderness survival and stealth.

mechanics.activation: 1 minute of preparation (no action economy cost once prepared, but requires a full minute).
mechanics.cost: Material component sourcing (mud, dirt, plants, soot, etc.).
mechanics.condition: Must be pressing against a solid surface (at least as tall and wide as the character) and must remain unmoving/not taking actions or reactions.
mechanics.limitations: The bonus immediately ends upon movement or taking an action/reaction, requiring re-application.

recommendations: The term 'try to hide' might be slightly ambiguous. Clarify if the initial Stealth check is made at the end of the minute or if the feature grants the bonus when a check *is* made while satisfying the conditions.
Ensure official errata or rulings confirm if 'taking actions' explicitly includes casting spells or making weapon attacks, which is usually the case, but explicit mention aids clarity.
